childNodes.textContent:
pm_idea-mockup.html — the PRD-phase MOCKUP for the pets-dev dev story.

  A self-contained, dependency-free HTML mockup of the proposed feature: a small
  animated SVG "pet" docked at the BOTTOM of the trace column in the kitsoki web
  drive view (VS Code Pets / tamagotchi style). It is emitted by the dev-story
  prd_published room (host.artifacts_dir, kind: slideshow) and embedded inline in
  kitsoki web via the media slideshow iframe, so the PM can see the mockup the PRD
  describes rendered in the run — no real renderer, no LLM.

  The mockup reproduces the real drive layout (chat | trace column = diagram over
  timeline, ref InteractiveView.vue / .iv__trace) and anchors the pet sprite at
  the column's bottom edge, exactly where the feature will live. The sprite is
  pure inline SVG + CSS keyframes — the same approach the real TracePet.vue will
  use: no images, no dependencies.

  Sandbox note: the host iframe is `sandbox="allow-scripts"` on an opaque origin,
  so everything here is inline (no external CSS/JS/fonts, no same-origin access).
Mockup · ambient companion in the trace column
A little pet at the bottom of the trace, keeping you company

A small animated SVG sprite docked at the bottom edge of the trace column. It idles and blinks while you watch a long run — a bit of warmth in the corner of the observer, taking no real estate from the trace itself. Pure inline SVG + CSS: no images, no dependencies, opt-in.
Add a little pet to the bottom of the trace column.
On it — adding an inline-SVG sprite anchored to the column bottom.

attributes.aria-label: trace column with pet docked at bottom
